collapseIdle.name: an empty log collapses to an empty log
collapseIdle.intent: collapseIdle is total: no events, no gaps, no shift. Mirrors the empty-trace parity the sibling projections pin.

collapseIdle.name: a single event is untouched
collapseIdle.intent: One event has no gap to measure; activeAt equals at. The identity mapping in its smallest form.

collapseIdle.name: a single sitting is the identity mapping, byte for byte
collapseIdle.intent: THE COMPAT PROOF (D29): no gap reaches SITTING_GAP_MS, so no gap collapses and every at is unchanged. Every analysis vector case that predates sittings is in this class, which is why the active-time re-base leaves them all byte-identical.

collapseIdle.name: a gap one millisecond under the threshold does not collapse
collapseIdle.intent: The boundary rule is >= SITTING_GAP_MS (1,800,000 ms). A 1,799,999 ms gap is under it: one sitting, identity mapping. The from-below edge of the threshold.

collapseIdle.name: a gap of exactly SITTING_GAP_MS is a boundary and collapses in full
collapseIdle.intent: The pinned tie: >= threshold splits. The whole 1,800,000 ms gap is subtracted, so the boundary events share one active instant (the seam), not a threshold-sized remainder.

collapseIdle.name: an overnight gap collapses to zero and the later sitting slides back whole
collapseIdle.intent: activeAt = at - (sum of collapsed gaps before it). The 28,800,000 ms (8 h) gap collapses in full: the last event of sitting 1 (at 300000) and the first of sitting 2 land on the same active instant 300000, and within-sitting spacing (60000 ms) is preserved.

collapseIdle.name: three sittings: each collapsed gap deepens the cumulative shift
collapseIdle.intent: Two boundaries (gaps of 2,000,000 and 3,600,000 ms). The shift accumulates: sitting 2 slides back by 2,000,000, sitting 3 by 5,600,000, so the three sittings concatenate at 0-120s, 120-180s, 180-240s on the active axis.

collapseIdle.name: clock skew never splits: a negative gap is below the threshold
collapseIdle.intent: Gaps are measured between consecutive events in seq order. Skew across writers puts a later-seq event at an earlier at, making the gap negative, which is under SITTING_GAP_MS: one sitting, identity mapping.

sittings.name: an empty log has no sittings
sittings.intent: count 0, no spans, wallSeconds 0. Total, though unreachable through the completed-only /analysis gate.

sittings.name: a single fill is one sitting with a zero-width span
sittings.intent: One event, one sitting. The trace is a single instant, so durationSeconds is 0: the span is [0, 0] and wallSeconds 0.

sittings.name: a single sitting is one span [0, durationSeconds] and wallSeconds equals it
sittings.intent: The single-sitting wire identity (D29): count 1, exactly one span covering the whole active axis, and wallSeconds == momentum.durationSeconds because nothing collapsed. A single-sitting game reads exactly as today.

sittings.name: one millisecond under the threshold stays one sitting, and nothing rounds
sittings.intent: The from-below edge on the wire: a 1,799,999 ms gap does not split. Pins the exact-division idiom too: the bundle reports 1799.999, unrounded (the engine subtracts and divides, it does not round), so span end and wallSeconds stay byte-equal to durationSeconds.

sittings.name: a gap of exactly SITTING_GAP_MS splits: two sittings, degenerate spans, wall kept for flavor
sittings.intent: The pinned tie on the wire: >= collapses. Both fills land on active instant 0, so durationSeconds is 0 and both spans are [0, 0], while wallSeconds keeps the 1800 s wall span for flavor copy.

sittings.name: two sittings across a night: contiguous spans, the seam at 300, the wall span at 29160
sittings.intent: The reference two-sitting solve (same log as collapseIdle's overnight case). Active duration 360 s; the boundary sits at active 300 s, so spans are [0, 300] and [300, 360]: spans[1].startSeconds == spans[0].endSeconds, the seam-tick contract. wallSeconds 29160 carries the eight hours for flavor only.

sittings.name: three sittings concatenate: [0,120], [120,180], [180,240]
sittings.intent: The reference three-sitting solve (same log as collapseIdle's cumulative-shift case). Each sitting starts exactly where the last ended; the last span ends at durationSeconds 240; wallSeconds 5840 is the wall trace span.

sittings.name: ACTIVITY IS ANY EVENT: a wrong write mid-gap keeps the sitting alive
sittings.intent: THE KEY CASE (D29). The first-correct trace alone has a 50-minute quiet stretch (0 to 3,000,000 ms), which would have split were the partition trace-based. The wrong write at 1,500,000 ms is presence, so the partition over the full event log sees gaps of 25 minutes, under the threshold: one sitting, full-width span, nothing collapsed.

sittings.name: the same two fills without the bridging event split into two sittings
sittings.intent: The contrast to the activity case: remove the mid-gap wrong write and the 3,000,000 ms gap is a boundary. Both fills share active instant 0, so the spans degenerate while wallSeconds keeps 3000 s. Presence is what bridges, not fills.

sittings.name: a fill-less first sitting clamps to a zero-width span at the axis origin
sittings.intent: Spans live on the trace's active axis (the ribbon's axis). A sitting of only wrong writes two hours before the solve contributes no trace entry; its boundary clamps to [0, durationSeconds], so it renders as a zero-width span at 0 while the count stays honest at 2. wallSeconds is the trace's wall span (60 s), not the log's.
